label: Superficial puncture wound without retained foreign body or infection
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of superficial puncture wound.
No retained foreign body, deep structure injury, infection, bite wound, dirty/high-risk wound, diabetic-foot risk, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion_criteria: Retained foreign body, deep puncture, tendon/nerve/vessel/bone concern, joint violation, open fracture, bite wound, dirty wound, infection, neurovascular compromise, severe pain, or delayed high-risk presentation.
Diabetic-foot risk, immunocompromise, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed wound/orthopedic plan.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, wound care, or the ED if the wound is not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports cuts and puncture wound framing, deep-structure risk, infection warning signs, and higher-risk wound categories such as bites, dirty wounds, crush injuries, and foot wounds. Autonomous Phase 2101-2200 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed superficial puncture wound without retained foreign body, deep structure injury, infection, high-risk wound context, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of superficial puncture wound
no retained foreign body, deep structure injury, infection, high-risk wound context, or specialist-directed plan documented
